macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Powell Warns of New Energy Supply Shock as Gas Prices Surge Above $4 Nationwide
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell cautioned about a potential energy supply shock amid surging gas prices hitting $4.11 on average due to Iran tensions and oil topping $100/barrel. Diesel prices in San Francisco exceeded a record $8/gallon for the first time.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls 2% for Fifth Straight Weekly Loss Amid Mideast Conflict
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% last week, marking five consecutive weekly losses for the first time since 2022, as elevated oil prices at $112/barrel for Brent crude fuel inflation concerns. U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% amid market jitters.

items.title: Fed's Bowman Pencils in Three Rate Cuts by End of 2026 Amid Labor Market Concerns
items.summary: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman indicated expectations for three rate cuts before the end of 2026, citing worries about the U.S. labor market. This comes as energy shocks from Middle East conflict pressure inflation.

items.title: Middle East War Drives Up Business Input Prices in ISM PMI Data
items.summary: The Institute for Supply Management’s Purchasing Managers Index showed business input prices surging 7.7 percentage points in March due to the Middle East war impacting inflation data. Bond markets remain volatile amid high oil prices.

items.title: Airlines Raise Baggage Fees as Jet Fuel Costs Soar from Iran Conflict
items.summary: United Airlines hiked checked bag fees by $10 and JetBlue by up to $9, citing surging jet fuel prices linked to the Iran war and global oil shortages. This reflects broader impacts on transportation costs.

items.title: Iran Strikes Halt Qatar LNG Production, Raising Global Supply Disruption Fears
items.summary: Iranian strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan facilities stopped major LNG production, leading to force majeure declarations and heightened concerns over energy supply disruptions worldwide.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: TRU Token Surges 98% Leading Crypto Gainers Amid High Volatility
items.summary: TRU led 24-hour gainers with a 98.46% surge to $0.0111 and $177.78M volume, followed by RED (+65.80%) and PCI (+27.12%). ARIAIP topped losers with a 49.24% drop to $0.0037.

items.title: Bitcoin Rebounds 3% to Nearly $70,000 Boosting Crypto Treasury Stocks
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 3% to nearly $70,000, driving 6% gains in Strategy (MSTR) and Bitmine (BMNR) stocks. Polymarket predicts high odds of further Bitcoin buys by Strategy.

items.title: $238 Million in Crypto Liquidations as Shorts Outpace Longs
items.summary: Crypto markets saw $238M in liquidations over 24 hours, with shorts losing $136M vs. longs' $103M. Bitcoin shorts liquidated for $68.42M, Ethereum shorts for $39.69M.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $70,000 on U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin broke $70,000 fueled by hopes of a 45-day U.S.-Iran ceasefire easing geopolitical tensions and oil prices. Institutional ETF inflows also support the rally.

items.title: Over 50% of XRP Supply Underwater After Losing 4th Largest Spot to BNB
items.summary: Only 43.4% of XRP supply is profitable at $1.33, down to July 2024 lows; BNB overtook XRP as 4th largest by market cap ($82.6B vs $82.1B). XRP rose 2.6% in 24h with $1.68M short liquidations.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Deploys AI Swarm to Map Tribal Knowledge in Data Pipelines
items.summary: Meta engineered a pre-compute engine using 50+ specialized AI agents to analyze over 4,100 code files across three languages, generating 59 context files that encode undocumented tribal knowledge. This boosts AI agent efficiency by 40% fewer tool calls and covers 100% of code modules.

items.title: AWS and Windward Launch Agentic Maritime Anomaly Analysis with Generative AI
items.summary: Windward integrated generative AI on AWS for contextual intelligence in maritime alerts, automating data collection from weather, news, and alerts while using Amazon Rerank for relevance filtering to streamline expert analysis.

items.title: Anthropic Cuts OpenClaw Access from Claude Subscriptions Amid Token Crunch
items.summary: Anthropic ended OpenClaw access in Claude subscriptions due to global token shortages, offering transition credits; Chinese AI firms clash over the exit while a CEO shares building AI employees on the platform.
items.source: South China Morning Post, ComputerWorld, Business Insider

items.title: Foxconn Achieves Record Q1 Sales Driven by AI Server Demand
items.summary: Foxconn reported highest-ever first-quarter sales of USD 66.62 billion, fueled by surging demand for AI servers.

items.title: OpenAI Advocates Robot Taxes and Public Wealth Fund for AI Job Losses
items.summary: OpenAI proposes robot taxes and a public wealth fund to mitigate economic impacts from AI-driven job displacement.

items.title: Iran Threatens to Destroy OpenAI’s $30B Stargate Data Center in UAE
items.summary: Iran issued threats against OpenAI's planned Stargate AI data center in Abu Dhabi, valued at $30 billion.

items.title: Google Cloud Rolls Out Conversational Analytics for Looker Embedded
items.summary: Google Cloud launched Conversational Analytics, enabling natural language queries in Looker Embedded environments.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Struggling with Declining Player Interest and Balancing Issues
items.summary: Marathon is reportedly dying shortly after launch, with souring player sentiment due to balancing problems and competition from older titles like Hunt Showdown outperforming it in daily peaks. Analysts suggest it has diverted resources from Destiny 2, potentially leading to studio challenges under Sony.

items.title: Crimson Desert Hardcore Fans Complain of Content Drought After 200+ Hours
items.summary: Less than a month after release, dedicated players have exhausted all killable content in Crimson Desert, leaving nothing to do as enemies do not respawn, unlike many open-world games.

items.title: Unity Gaming Report 2026 Highlights Shift Toward Small Indie Games and AI Trends
items.summary: The new Unity Gaming Report emphasizes industry moves toward shorter, smaller indie hits like Vampire Survivors and Balatro, with growing focus on AI and opportunities for solo developers.

items.title: ARC Raiders Receives Trials Update with New Damage Vaporizers Task
items.summary: The latest update for ARC Raiders introduces a trials mode task focused on damaging vaporizers, with guides emerging on optimal strategies and locations.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Achieves Massive Box Office Success
items.summary: The Super Mario Galaxy movie has grossed around $370 million worldwide in five days, tripling its $110 million production budget (excluding marketing), marking it a major hit for Universal and Nintendo.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Jack Dorsey's Bitchat App Removed from Apple's App Store in China
items.summary: Apple removed Jack Dorsey's Bluetooth P2P messaging app Bitchat from the App Store in China at the request of the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC). The app, launched in July last year, has been used during protests in Madagascar, Uganda, Nepal, Indonesia, and Iran.

items.title: Wipro Acquires Mindsprint for $375M and Signs $1B+ Contract with Olam
items.summary: Indian IT giant Wipro agreed to acquire Mindsprint, the IT services arm of Singapore-based Olam, for $375 million, and struck an eight-year contract worth over $1 billion with Olam. Wipro shares rose as much as 3.2% following the announcement.

items.title: Netflix Launches Playground Games App for Young Children
items.summary: Netflix launched Netflix Playground, a games app designed for kids aged eight and under, in the US, UK, Canada, Australia, the Philippines, and New Zealand.

items.title: Microsoft Forces Windows 11 24H2 Devices to Update to 25H2 Without Full Opt-Out
items.summary: Microsoft is updating Windows 11 24H2 devices to version 25H2 using an 'intelligent' ML-based rollout system, with no complete way to opt out.

items.title: Samsung Discontinuing Samsung Messages App, Urging Switch to Google Messages
items.summary: Samsung is officially ending support for its Messages app and instructing users to switch to Google Messages.

items.title: Linux 7.1 Ends Support for Intel's 37-Year-Old 486 Processor
items.summary: Linux kernel 7.1 is dropping support for Intel's 486 processor, which is now 37 years old.
